# GembaEscrow

> Smart-contract escrow for real estate and high-value asset transactions. Non-custodial, multi-party approval, timeout refunds. Part of the GEMBA IT ecosystem.

GembaEscrow removes the trusted-third-party from real estate deposits and deal completion. Buyer, seller, and (optional) agent/notary each approve critical steps on-chain. Funds auto-release on consensus, or refund to the buyer on timeout. No escrow company takes a cut — only base network gas applies.

## URLs

- Home: https://escrow.gembait.com/en

Languages: English (en), Bulgarian (bg), Spanish (es). Replace `/en` with `/bg` or `/es`.

## Quick facts

- Network: GembaBlockchain Testnet testnet (chain ID 11155111)
- Model: Non-custodial — no custodian or platform holds funds in trust
- Parties: Buyer, seller, agent/notary (optional)
- Fee model: Base network gas only — no platform cut on escrowed funds
- Timeout behavior: If the deadline passes without multi-party approval, the buyer reclaims the full deposit
- Status: Testnet demonstration; mainnet deployment on request

## Typical flow

1. Buyer, seller, and (optional) notary are defined in a new deal on-chain
2. Buyer deposits funds into the escrow contract
3. Notary verifies off-chain documents, then signals on-chain approval
4. On multi-party approval, contract atomically splits funds to seller, agent, and notary
5. If the deadline elapses without approval, buyer calls refund() and reclaims the deposit

## Technology

- Frontend: React, Vite, Tailwind CSS, i18next (en/bg/es)
- Web3: ethers.js, wagmi, viem
- Contracts: Solidity 0.8.x on GembaBlockchain, OpenZeppelin primitives, ReentrancyGuard

## Built by

GEMBA IT — https://gembait.com
Parent company: GEMBA EOOD (Varna, Bulgaria)
Contact: contacts@gembait.com
